lead_profile_text: Selling property; wants STL in the meantime while waiting for property market to improve. Three options considered: sell, long-term let, short-term rental. Currently for sale but won't go below a certain price — market conditions poor due to new legislation. Had same tenant (teacher) for 5–6 years; well-maintained. Owners from Poland. Potential contractor partnership via Mateusz's installation company.
emotional_profile: Motivated seller — sees STL as optimal way to generate income while waiting for market improvement. Personal connection to Central European market angle. Flexible and open but wants clarity on costs, guest quality, and access rights.
delay_trigger_phrases: We don't want to go below a certain price
market conditions poor due to new legislation
want flexibility to use property occasionally and sell if good offer comes
delay_timeline: Property actively for sale; STL is interim income strategy while waiting for market to improve. 3-month exit clause after initial term provides sell flexibility.

top_objections.text: Income consistency — wants to understand the full cost breakdown more clearly
top_objections.zac_response: Estimated net revenue £14,796 annually (£1,233/month avg); total deductions ~45% (management 13%, cleaning ~18%, platform fees ~15%) explained

top_objections.text: Wrong type of guests — worried about the calibre of guests staying in the property
top_objections.zac_response: £200 security deposit, ID checks, £100k damage insurance presented as concrete vetting measures; Central European market angle as a guest quality signal

top_objections.text: 72-hour access notice — raised as concern about flexibility to access own property
top_objections.zac_response: Not resolved on call — needs explicit written explanation of when the 72-hour access clause applies and how personal use blocking works

top_objections.text: Market conditions — general uncertainty about whether now is the right time to STL
top_objections.zac_response: STL framed as interim strategy while market improves; 3-month notice after initial term provides exit flexibility if good sale offer arrives

top_questions.text: What are the total costs — how does 13% management + cleaning + platform fees add up?
top_questions.answer: Total deductions ~45%: management 13%+VAT, cleaning ~18%, platform fees ~15%. Net £1,233/month average (£14,796/year).

top_questions.text: What is the 72-hour access notice rule — can I still access my own property?
top_questions.answer: Not fully addressed on call — follow-up required with written explanation

what_moved_them: Direct booking website (multi-platform approach across Airbnb, Booking.com, direct site)
Central European guest market angle — personal connection as Polish owners
£200 security deposit + ID checks + £100k damage insurance (directly addressed guest quality concern)
Slack channel and monthly statements (transparency and control)
Flexibility to block for personal use and sell with 3-month notice after initial term
Potential contractor partnership opportunity via Mateusz's installation company

conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Provide clear written explanation of the 72-hour access clause, when it applies, and how personal use blocking interacts with it

conversion_gaps.gap: Cost breakdown not fully absorbed — total ~45% deductions not clearly understood
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Send detailed cost breakdown showing gross-to-net calculation for their specific property figures (£1,233/month net from ~£2,240 gross)

offer_framing: 13%+VAT (reduced from 15%) if signed by 31 May 2026. Follow-up call 22 May 2026 at 16:00 via Google Meet.
